subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Differential Equation $dy/dx = y/x$ — Slope Field, Particular Solution & Concavity

parts.prompt: On the axes provided, sketch a slope field for the given differential equation at the eight points indicated. (Note: Use the axes provided in the pink exam booklet.)
parts.scoring_notes: Slope field: at any point (x,y) the slope is y/x. In Q1 (x>0,y>0) and Q3 (x<0,y<0) slopes are positive; in Q2 (x<0,y>0) and Q4 (x>0,y<0) slopes are negative. At points on the axes the slope is 0 or undefined.

scoring_points.description: Correct zero slopes at points where $y = 0$ (slopes are $0/x = 0$)
Correct sign pattern: positive slopes in Q1 and Q3, negative slopes in Q2 and Q4

required_elements: Positive slopes in Q1 ($x>0, y>0$) and Q3 ($x<0, y<0$)
Negative slopes in Q2 ($x<0, y>0$) and Q4 ($x>0, y<0$)

parts.prompt: Find the particular solution $y = f(x)$ to the differential equation with the initial condition $f(-1) = 1$ and state its domain.
parts.scoring_notes: Separate variables: dy/y = dx/x. Integrate: ln|y| = ln|x| + C. Exponentiate: |y| = e^C |x|, so y = Ax for constant A. Apply f(-1)=1: 1 = A(-1) => A = -1. Particular solution: y = -x. Domain: x < 0 (since f(-1) = 1 > 0 and y = -x > 0 requires x < 0).

scoring_points.description: Correct antiderivative of right side: $\ln|x| + C$
Uses initial condition $f(-1) = 1$ to find $A = -1$, giving $y = -x$

alternatives.correct_example: Since $y = -x$ and the solution passes through $(-1, 1)$ (where $x < 0$), the domain is $x < 0$.

common_traps: Stating domain as all real numbers or $x \ne 0$ without restricting to the branch containing the initial condition

parts.prompt: Find $\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}$ in terms of $x$ and $y$. Determine the concavity of the particular solution found in part (b) at $x = -1$. Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: Differentiate dy/dx = y/x implicitly: d^2y/dx^2 = (x*y' - y)/x^2 = (x*(y/x) - y)/x^2 = (y - y)/x^2 = 0... wait: (x*(y/x) - y)/x^2 = (y - y)/x^2 = 0. That gives 0 which is not helpful. Actually use quotient rule: d/dx[y/x] = (xy' - y)/x^2 = (x*(y/x) - y)/x^2 = (y - y)/x^2 = 0? Let me recalculate: d^2y/dx^2 = d/dx[y/x] = (x * dy/dx - y * 1)/x^2 = (x*(y/x) - y)/x^2 = (y - y)/x^2 = 0. So d^2y/dx^2 = 0? For particular solution y = -x: y'' = 0 (linear). Concavity is neither concave up nor concave down (linear).

scoring_points.description: Correctly differentiates $\frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{y}{x}$ with respect to $x$: $\frac{d^2y}{dx^2} = \frac{x \frac{dy}{dx} - y}{x^2} = \frac{x \cdot \frac{y}{x} - y}{x^2} = \frac{y - y}{x^2} = 0$
At $x = -1$, $\frac{d^2y}{dx^2} = 0$, so the particular solution is neither concave up nor concave down (it is linear: $y = -x$)

required_elements: Neither concave up nor concave down (linear)
alternatives.correct_example: $y'' = 0$ everywhere, so the solution $y = -x$ is linear — neither concave up nor concave down at $x = -1$.
